1.description: Stock borrowing fees for specific listings. Panel A of this figure plots the daily option-implied borrowing fee and the indicative fee for Tesla Motors. Panel B of this figure plots the daily option-implied borrowing fee and the indicative fee for Sears Holdings. The option-implied fees are adjusted to account for early exercise using the procedure described in SectionI. The filters used to identify the stocks with at least one valid put-call pair to calculate the option-implied fee are described in SectionII. The sample period is from July 2006 or initial public offering (whichever is later) to August 2015.

2.description: Stock borrowing fees and borrowing fee risk premium. This figure plots the fitted values from kernel regressions of the indicative borrowing fee, adjusted option-implied fee, and borrowing fee risk premium on utilization. The unit of observation is the combination of a stock and trading date for the optionable subset of common stocks in CRSP that match to a valid put-call pair in Optionmetrics and an indicative borrowing fee in Markit. The computation of the adjusted option-implied borrowing fee is described in SectionII. The borrowing fee risk premium is the difference between the adjusted option-implied fee and the average realized indicative fee during the life of the put-call pairs used to compute the option-implied fee. The filters used to identify the stocks with at least one valid put-call pair to calculate the option-implied fee are described in SectionI. The sample period is July 2006 to August 2015. The kernel regressions were estimated using the Stata function lpoly, with the default options of local mean smoothing and the Epanechnikov kernel.
